source.title: Thalamic circuits for independent control of prefrontal signal and noise

confidence_rationale: Direct evidence that MD thalamus gates prefrontal processing via two distinct projection types (signal amplification vs noise suppression); supports ARC-023's claim that MD thalamus is the gate for the E3 planning-gates loop. Supports TRN-as-inter-loop-router via established inhibitory TRN architecture. Main caveat: the paper addresses signal/noise control in a single loop, not multi-rate coordination across three loops.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The mediodorsal (MD) thalamus projects two distinct pathways to prefrontal cortex: one (D2-expressing) amplifies PFC signals when inputs are sparse, and another (GRIK4-expressing) suppresses PFC noise when inputs are dense/conflicting; the thalamic reticular nucleus (TRN) provides inhibitory gating of all thalamocortical relay nuclei, routing information between loops.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-023: MD thalamus is the biological substrate of the E3 (planning-gates loop) heartbeat pacemaker — consistent with MD gating PFC deliberative processing. The TRN serves as the inter-loop routing switch between E1, E2, E3 thalamic relay nuclei (sensory relays, VL motor thalamus, MD prefrontal thalamus), gating cross-loop communication at each heartbeat tick. MECH-089 (theta-buffer cross-rate integration): the thalamic relay structure provides the physical architecture for the ThetaBuffer — MD gates E3 access to theta-cycle summaries of E2/E1 output.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Nature 2021 paper demonstrates within-PFC signal/noise control by MD but does not directly test multi-rate coordination across three distinct cortico-thalamic loops. The VL (E2 motor loop) and sensory relay (E1 loop) components of ARC-023 require separate corroboration — see also motor thalamus literature for VL. The multi-rate heartbeat architecture (SD-006) remains V3-scoped and untested.
mapping.source_context: Mouse prefrontal cortex circuit dissection; viral projection tracing, optogenetics, and cognitive task performance.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality mechanistic study; mapping to MD-as-E3-gate is well-supported; multi-rate and three-loop architecture aspects are extrapolations beyond the source scope.
